message:
docs(Cargo.toml): correct version-split comment — PARTIALLY resolved, not fully

My prior commit (d8f20a3b) rewrote this block to claim the
asupersync version split was fully resolved. That was aspirational:
the direct cass dep moved to crates.io asupersync 0.3.0, but the
lockfile still carries two older 0.2.9 copies (the frankensqlite
crates.io transitive and the frankensearch git-rev 08dd31df
transitive). The comment now matches reality and documents the
conditions under which the remaining two copies collapse into one.

- Cargo.toml: rewrite the "Asupersync version split" block. Header
  changes "now resolved by moving every source" →
  "PARTIALLY resolved" with an explicit breakdown of what's done
  (direct cass dep → 0.3.0, no more git rev pin) versus what's
  still at 0.2.9 (frankensqlite registry transitive, frankensearch
  git-rev transitive) and the release-wave condition under which
  the remaining copies disappear. The local-dev [patch.crates-io]
  template and CI-guard warning both stay unchanged.

No functional change; comment-only.

files.patch:
@@ -164,12 +164,22 @@ inherits = "release"
 debug = true
 strip = false
 
-# Asupersync version split — now resolved by moving every source (direct
-# dep, frankensqlite transitive, frankensearch transitive) onto crates.io
-# asupersync 0.3.0. If you need to build against a local checkout of
-# asupersync (sibling at ../asupersync) during runtime-level hacking,
-# add the following [patch.crates-io] block LOCALLY and DO NOT COMMIT IT —
-# the fresh-clone CI guard (.github/workflows/fresh-clone-build.yml) will
+# Asupersync version split — PARTIALLY resolved. The direct cass dep
+# is now on crates.io asupersync 0.3.0 (no more git rev pin), but the
+# lockfile still carries two older asupersync 0.2.9 copies pulled in
+# transitively:
+#   - registry copy via frankensqlite (crates.io frankensqlite still
+#     depends on asupersync 0.2.9)
+#   - git rev 08dd31df via frankensearch (this project's frankensearch
+#     dep uses rev db7530e5, which still references asupersync 0.2.9)
+# Both collapse into a single asupersync 0.3.0 entry once frankensqlite
+# and frankensearch cut matching 0.3-era releases on crates.io and we
+# bump the deps here to consume them.
+#
+# If you need to build against a local checkout of asupersync (sibling
+# at ../asupersync) during runtime-level hacking, add the following
+# [patch.crates-io] block LOCALLY and DO NOT COMMIT IT — the
+# fresh-clone CI guard (.github/workflows/fresh-clone-build.yml) will
 # fail if any [patch] block pointing at sibling paths is committed:
 #
 # [patch.crates-io]
